inline_comments.message:
this would actually fail since the blueprint was already uploaded.

Instead you want a 
cfy deployments create -b ves-tls -d ves-tls

The "cfy install" command is actually a macros that combines the two commands "cfy blueprints upload" and "cfy deployments create"

the "cfy uninstall" command is a macro that combines multiple commands together, including the "cfy blueprint delete" command shown here. That means that running it (again) will fail because it will have already been deleted.

The blueprint id for default bootstrap install is ves, the new bp/deploy will be a parallel instance to be setup. Hence there will not be a conflict.

I've validated these steps on SB04 and DCAE tenants. Instead of doing separate deployment create/execution steps, the single installation command will suffice.
Yes, there is timing issue though. When we try cfy uninstall - I've seen blueprint delete failing (likely container CM thing) hence needing a separate step to delete.

yes, uninstall will occasionally fail to delete the blueprint. So change

"- Delete blueprint"

to

"- If uninstall fails to delete the blueprint, delete it with this:"
